title: Expand evidence cards to a second cancer indication and target panel (only after M2 stable; full source-vetting + scientific sign-off)
context: After the first target panel and cancer indication are stable (M2 complete, scientific sign-off recorded, compliance gate green), the project may expand to a second cancer indication and target panel. This is a backlog/future task: breadth never precedes verified accuracy for the first set. The second indication must go through the full selection criteria (rare cancers prioritised, Ewing/sarcoma excluded), per-source licence verification, card assembly, and scientific reviewer sign-off — the same process as M0-M2. This is not in scope for v1.
objective: Select and assemble research evidence cards for a second cancer indication and target panel, applying the full M0-M2 process: scored indication/panel selection (rare cancers prioritised, Ewing/sarcoma excluded), per-source licence verification for the new indication's sources, card assembly with both-axes grading and contradiction surfacing, compliance gate pass, and version-scoped scientific-reviewer sign-off before release.
acceptanceCriteria: Task does not start until M2 (cards-010 and review-011) is stable and the first panel's cards have been publicly released and used by at least one beneficiary

Second cancer indication and target panel selected using the same scored criteria as task 003 (rare cancers prioritised, Ewing/sarcoma excluded, open-data coverage, available scientific reviewer, tractable scope)
Per-source licence verification completed for all sources feeding the second indication (same process as sources-009); COSMIC/OncoKB excluded; open-sa sources flagged for governance decision

Evidence cards assembled for the second panel with every assertion cited and graded on both axes (evidenceQuality x evidenceMaturity), targetSafety populated, contradictions surfaced with taxonomy tags
All cards pass the compliance gate and schema validation in CI; build manifest emitted
Version-scoped scientific-reviewer sign-off recorded for each released card before publication; inter-rater Cohen's kappa measured and reported
output: An expanded evidence card dataset covering a second cancer indication and target panel, with full provenance, grading, compliance gate pass, and scientific sign-off — extending the project's research value after the first panel is stable.
